results.title: Hot this afternoon for Southeast Georgia, Lowcountry, plus tracking Bertha
results.description: Hot, hazy, and humid again in Southeast Georgia and the Lowcountry, with feels-like temps near 105°. Bertha is also tracking toward the Texas coast.

results.title: Comedian MC Lively Set To Wed Fiancée Dumebi JamesComedian MC Lively Set To Wed Fiancée Dumebi James By Zuleihat Chatta Popular Nigerian comedian, actor, and content creator, Michael Sani Amanesi, professionally known as MC Lively, is set to tie the knot with his fiancée, Dumebi James. The popular entertainer officially announced the news on social media alongside a series of pre-wedding photographs, giving fans a glimpse of the couple ahead of their upcoming nuptials. MC Lively first rose to national prominence through his viral comedic skits featuring his signature character “Barrister Mike,” which satirized post-graduation joblessness and real-life Nigerian experiences. A trained lawyer, the 33-year-old comedian graduated with a law degree from Obafemi Awolowo University and was called to the Nigerian Bar in 2016 before pivoting full-time into the creative industry. Over the years, the entertainer has expanded his footprint beyond digital content, starring in Nollywood feature films such as Fate of Alakada, Seven and a Half Dates, and Breaded Life. Following the announcement, colleagues, fans, and well-wishers have flooded the actor’s social media pages with congratulations and warm wishes as the couple prepares for their wedding celebrations.

results.title: Nagaland University partners with Japanese farms to boost agricultural training
results.description: Kohima: Nagaland University has entered into partnerships with two agricultural firms in Japan to provide its students with hands-on experience in modern farming techniques and an opportunity to gain exposure to Japanese workplace practices. results.title: Son of ex-Chinese official jailed for 6 years, 9 months for deception, laundering
results.description: The son of a former mainland Chinese official has been jailed for six years and nine months for using deceptive means to secure residency in Hong Kong and laundering more than HK$64 million, including a bribe his father allegedly sought a decade ago. The District Court on Thursday sentenced Xiao Rui, 37, on four counts of money laundering and one of using false instruments for offences he committed between 2013 and 2023. Deputy Judge Bernard Chung Wai-keung said Xiao had undermined the...

results.title: Sensex, Nifty fall for fourth day, investors lose Rs 3 lakh crore as crude surges
results.description: Mumbai: Indian stock markets declined for the fourth consecutive session on Thursday as escalating tensions in West Asia pushed crude oil prices towards $99 per barrel and triggered concerns over inflation and India’s import bill. The 30-share BSE Sensex fell 363.66 points, or 0.47%, to close at 76,391.39. The NSE Nifty 50 declined 126.65 points, or 0.53%, to settle at 23,869.60, slipping below the 23,900 mark. Over the four-session losing streak, the Sensex has declined around 1,760 points, or 2.3%, while the Nifty has fallen about 465 points, or nearly 2%. Investors lost around Rs 3 lakh crore on Thursday as the total market capitalisation of BSE-listed companies dropped to Rs 476.85 lakh crore. It stood at around Rs 480 lakh crore in the previous session. The cumulative value of BSE-listed companies has fallen by over Rs 4 lakh crore during the four-day market decline. Selling was more pronounced in the broader market. The Nifty Midcap 100 and Nifty Smallcap 100 indices declined around 1% each, indicating widespread risk aversion among investors. Market breadth was also negative, with 2,585 stocks declining against 1,637 advances on the BSE. Another 169 shares closed unchanged. Crude oil surge hits sentiment The decline was primarily driven by the sharp rise in international crude oil prices following fresh threats to key shipping routes in West Asia. Brent crude September futures jumped sharply and briefly approached $99 per barrel as investors assessed the risk of further disruption to global energy supplies. Oil prices rose after Iran-backed Houthi rebels attacked two Saudi oil tankers and threatened to block Saudi-linked shipping through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait. The development raised concerns that supply disruptions could spread beyond the Strait of Hormuz to another strategically important oil and trade route. As India imports most of its crude-oil requirements, a sustained rise in prices could increase the country’s import bill, add to inflationary pressure and weaken the rupee. It may also affect corporate earnings by raising transportation, fuel and input costs. The rupee declined four paise to provisionally close at 96.57 against the US dollar. The domestic currency traded between 96.45 and 96.59 during the session. Among Sensex constituents, Adani Ports, Bajaj Finance, IndiGo, Axis Bank and Reliance Industries were among the major laggards. Mahindra and Mahindra, TCS, Eternal, HCL Technologies and Bajaj Finserv ended among the gainers. Realty, banking, chemicals and oil and gas shares faced selling pressure, while automobile stocks outperformed the broader market.

results.title: Top Gear's The Stig opens up on plans to sue BBC for £100k after horror Freddie Flintoff crash
results.description: Top Gear's The Stig has revealed he is planning to sue the BBC for £100,000 after claiming to have been badly hurt in the crash on the BBC show that left Andrew 'Freddie' Flintoff with severe injuries. Paul Rees, 41, claimed to The Sun that he broke his back in the horrifying crash which took place during filming for the series in 2022, while also accusing the BBC of failing to adhere to proper safety measures. Recounting the moment of the horror collision, he described to the publication how his vision went "red" from Mr Flintoff's blood "spraying me in the face." He continued: "I remember thinking that if we can get out of this and be in wheelchairs, we're lucky." Mr Rees claimed he had informed the BBC of potential risks but was "not listened to." “I genuinely felt like we were going to potentially die, because we had no protection," he recalled. "I'm used to ­having four or five-point harnesses, a helmet, and a roll hoop. I was acutely aware the only thing holding my head off the tarmac was Fred’s head. I still have no idea how I walked away." The married father-of-two says he broke his back and was left with PTSD following the ordeal. Mr Flintoff, 48, was left in a serious condition following the crash and underwent facial reconstructive surgeries before receiving a £12million payout. Mr Rees, who was The Stig on the show from 2019 to 2022, is now pushing for £100k in compensation but stated he would have accepted an apology from the broadcaster. He claimed that, before the crash, Mr Flintoff had been instructed to wear comedy glasses and a racing cap, leaving Mr Rees to question why there were "no helmets." Recalling the moment the accident happened, he added: "I said to [Mr Flintoff] power, power, power, power. He’s given it a load of power, which is what we did the time before. "And suddenly, because it had this low scoop and I had my arm hanging out the side, the next thing I know we are on top of my arm sliding down the runway. LATEST DEVELOPMENTS Katie Price's husband faces fresh blow as his own family speak out against him: 'Left us shocked' Jeremy Clarkson admits 'truly horrifying' scenes have been filmed for upcoming Clarkson's Farm James Norton recalls chaos of suffering medical emergency at star-studded event: 'Pandemonium!' "We're effectively just inverting with our faces going straight towards the tarmac, and then we're upside down. It's because Fred had taken the impact. Fred was hunched over on top of me. I pulled him back next to me, took one look at him and I thought he was dead. "I thought there was no way someone could take an injury to the face like that and survive. I could hear a gurgling noise, and I could see his chest going up and down. It was like a war injury, something you see in the movies." He stated that production had opted to use one trained medic on the scene rather than a full ambulance crew. "I'll never forget the first words he said, 'I’m not equipped for this injury and situation.' We used to have a full-size ambulance on set at all times, which was usually where I got changed into the white Stig suit. "Suddenly, to have a guy in a small hatchback who, the first thing he says when he gets on the scene is 'I'm not prepared'... It's worrying." He shared how he called 999 but was told the nearest ambulance would take two hours. Thankfully, soldiers training nearby had seen the accident and offered to get an air ambulance. Mr Rees went on to claim he didn't hear from the BBC again until he was offered a group therapy session with other staff. It wasn't until 2023 that Mr Rees allegedly discovered he had broken his back after months of pain. Reflecting on how the BBC had called him to London for a meeting, the Top Gear star claimed the broadcaster had tried to blame him for what happened, leaving him feeling like he was "being thrown under the bus." Speaking to GB News, a spokesperson from the BBC stated: "We dispute this claim and are defending it. As it's now before the courts, it would be inappropriate to comment further." Our Standards: The GB News Editorial Charter
